text: vv. 6-8: The words ‘and had intended to try him according to our own law’, ‘and insisted that his accusers must appear before thee’, are wanting in some manuscripts; others omit the intervening words as well, so that the passage reads ‘We arrested him; interrogate him, and thou wilt be able to learn the truth about all the accusations we bring against him’. As the text stands, it is not quite certain whether Felix is asked to interrogate St Paul, or Claudius Lysias; probably the former (but cf. verse 22 below).
Some manuscripts read ‘would offer him a bribe for his release’, which is evidently the meaning of the passage.
